text:
Market today: The mood is cautious due to Dallas Fed comments suggesting higher interest rates. This is the real driver, as rising rates typically pressure growth and tech stocks, which dominate your book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-45.65%) and SPAL (-41.67%) are both leveraged ETFs with significant losses and stops well above current prices, indicating a need for urgent review. SMH (-11.23%) is also flagged "SELL." Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further Fed commentary on rates and how your leveraged positions react.

Bottom line: Focus on risk management and re-evaluating stops on your most volatile, leveraged positions.
plays: Review SOXL and SPAL: Both leveraged ETFs are trading significantly below their listed stops. Adjust these stops to current risk tolerance or consider trimming to reduce exposure to decay and leverage.
Consider trimming SMH: With a "SELL" status and -11.23% loss, trimming SMH could reduce chip sector concentration and free up margin.
No new buys today - hold and watch: Rate hike talk and existing leverage warrant caution; no compelling new tech buys from the scanner with a strong external catalyst.
